module1.clean_text:
READMISSION SHEET
This is the child [PATIENT] aged [AGE] originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, with a personal history of a DIP type common variable immunodeficiency admitted to our service for the 9th treatment of venoglobulin. Reason for hospitalization: 9th course of venoglobulin. HISTORY:
Family:
Father: [AGE] years old, BES. Mother: [AGE] years old, BES. Siblings: daughter aged [AGE] with asthma, screening in progress
Inbreeding: no
Atopy: yes
Chronic respiratory disease in the family: no
Personal:
Physiological:
Pregnancy: well monitored
Delivery: at term by upper route
Apgar 10/10Neonatal resuscitation: no
Birth weight: 3.7kg
Meconium: emitted<24h
No delay in umbilical cord fall
Breastfeeding: mixed from birth. Vaccination: correctly vaccinated
No delay in psychomotor development
Nursery: no
Socio-economic level: good
Personal atopy: no
Diversification at the age of [AGE] D received at the age of [AGE] Pathological:
Hospitalization in the EHS CANASTEL infectious disease department on [DATE] for the case of a prolonged fever attributed to an EBV infection
2nd hospitalization on [DATE] in the same department for a return of fever with a rash
Environment:
Habitat: not humid
No animals
No passive smoking
History of the disease:
Date of diagnosis: [DATE] of diagnosis: 8 years
Diagnostic elements:
Clinic:
Prolonged fever
Atypical febrile rash
Paraclinic:
Active replication of EBV: EBV viral load positive 3.3 log, aevc IgG EBNA and VCA + IgM neg
Specific dosage of serum proteins on [DATE] at LILLE hospital:
IgM 0.32 g/L
IgA 0.55
IgG 3.5 g/L
Immunodeficiency assessment done on [DATE] in BENI MESOUS ALGIERS:
Pan-hypogammaglobulinemia associated with a virtual absence of B lymphocytes. cellular immunity is preserved. 2nd assessment of immunodeficiency carried out on 22/06/2025 in BENI MESOUS ALGIERS: confirms the diagnosis: common variable immunodeficiency. Hospitalization histories:
PROCESSING IN PROGRESS:
Bactrim (40/200) at preventive dose + Itraconazole 05mg/kg/day
EVENTS SINCE LAST PASSAGE:
Concept of fever treated by symptomatic treatment
No rash
No respiratory or ENT damage
No hospitalizations or consultations
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 24kg500 (+0.24 DS): Weight gain of 300g
Size: 127cm(-0.23DS)
BMI: 15.1(-0.34DS) between P25-50
Gomez score: 88%
Waterloo Score: 89%
General condition: preserved, T° 37.2 c, TCNC
Skin examination: CV (BCG) present, TCNC, no skin lesion
ENT examination: no cervical ADP, clean throat. Pleuropulmonary examination:
FR 25 c/min
	No signs of respiratory distress. SPO2: 98% AA
Pulmonary auscultation: clear at 2CP
no signs of chronic respiratory insufficiency (finger clubbing), no thoracic deformity. Cardiovascular examination: HR: 110 b/min. Auscultation: B1B2 audible no murmurs or BSA
Digestive examination: soft abdomen no organomegaly, transit preserved: 1 stool/day with normal appearance. Urogenital examination: Male morphotype, no abnormalities of sexual differentiation, Tanner stage 1
Review of other devices: is unremarkable
Residual IgG level dosage (11/18/2025): 5.98g/l
CAT:
Hospitalization. Conditioning. VVP. A course of immunoglobulin: dose of 0.8 mg/kg/4h with oral hyperhydration
Monitoring: CHMD, blood sugar, temperature. Evolution:
The treatment passed without incident
The patient discharged after the end of the treatment with an appointment for his next venoglobulin treatment on 01/13/2026
CONCLUSION
This is the child BENAMEUR SID AHMED aged 08, originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, with a personal history of a PID type common variable immunodeficiency admitted to our service for a 9th course of venoglobulins passed without incident. Exams requested:
- Request an immune deficiency assessment for the sister as part of the screening

inconsistencies.message: Female-specific term 'pregnancy' found in male patient record
Female-specific term 'cervical' found in male patient record

ordo_candidates.name: Mendelian susceptibility to mycobacterial diseases due to partial IRF8 deficiency

phenopacket.input:
READMISSION SHEET
This is the child BENAMEUR SID AHMED aged 08, originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, with a personal history of a DIP type common variable immunodeficiency admitted to our service for the 9th treatment of venoglobulin. Reason for hospitalization: 9th course of venoglobulin. HISTORY:
Family:
Father: 46 years old, BES. Mother: 34 years old, BES. Siblings: daughter aged 09 with asthma, screening in progress
Inbreeding: no
Atopy: yes
Chronic respiratory disease in the family: no
Personal:
Physiological:
Pregnancy: well monitored
Delivery: at term by upper route
Apgar 10/10Neonatal resuscitation: no
Birth weight: 3.7kg
Meconium: emitted<24h
No delay in umbilical cord fall
Breastfeeding: mixed from birth. Vaccination: correctly vaccinated
No delay in psychomotor development
Nursery: no
Socio-economic level: good
Personal atopy: no
Diversification at the age of 5 months
Vitamin D received at the age of 01 months/06 months. Pathological:
Hospitalization in the EHS CANASTEL infectious disease department on 06/02/2025 for the case of a prolonged fever attributed to an EBV infection
2nd hospitalization on 04/2025 in the same department for a return of fever with a rash
Environment:
Habitat: not humid
No animals
No passive smoking
History of the disease:
Date of diagnosis: 04/22/2025
Age of diagnosis: 8 years
Diagnostic elements:
Clinic:
Prolonged fever
Atypical febrile rash
Paraclinic:
Active replication of EBV: EBV viral load positive 3.3 log, aevc IgG EBNA and VCA + IgM neg
Specific dosage of serum proteins on 04/22/2025 at LILLE hospital:
IgM 0.32 g/L
IgA 0.55
IgG 3.5 g/L
Immunodeficiency assessment done on 13/05/2025 in BENI MESOUS ALGIERS:
Pan-hypogammaglobulinemia associated with a virtual absence of B lymphocytes. cellular immunity is preserved. 2nd assessment of immunodeficiency carried out on 22/06/2025 in BENI MESOUS ALGIERS: confirms the diagnosis: common variable immunodeficiency. Hospitalization histories:
PROCESSING IN PROGRESS:
Bactrim (40/200) at preventive dose + Itraconazole 05mg/kg/day
EVENTS SINCE LAST PASSAGE:
Concept of fever treated by symptomatic treatment
No rash
No respiratory or ENT damage
No hospitalizations or consultations
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 24kg500 (+0.24 DS): Weight gain of 300g
Size: 127cm(-0.23DS)
BMI: 15.1(-0.34DS) between P25-50
Gomez score: 88%
Waterloo Score: 89%
General condition: preserved, T° 37.2 c, TCNC
Skin examination: CV (BCG) present, TCNC, no skin lesion
ENT examination: no cervical ADP, clean throat. Pleuropulmonary examination:
FR 25 c/min
	No signs of respiratory distress. SPO2: 98% AA
Pulmonary auscultation: clear at 2CP
no signs of chronic respiratory insufficiency (finger clubbing), no thoracic deformity. Cardiovascular examination: HR: 110 b/min. Auscultation: B1B2 audible no murmurs or BSA
Digestive examination: soft abdomen no organomegaly, transit preserved: 1 stool/day with normal appearance. Urogenital examination: Male morphotype, no abnormalities of sexual differentiation, Tanner stage 1
Review of other devices: is unremarkable
Residual IgG level dosage (11/18/2025): 5.98g/l
CAT:
Hospitalization. Conditioning. VVP. A course of immunoglobulin: dose of 0.8 mg/kg/4h with oral hyperhydration
Monitoring: CHMD, blood sugar, temperature. Evolution:
The treatment passed without incident
The patient discharged after the end of the treatment with an appointment for his next venoglobulin treatment on 01/13/2026
CONCLUSION
This is the child BENAMEUR SID AHMED aged 08, originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, with a personal history of a PID type common variable immunodeficiency admitted to our service for a 9th course of venoglobulins passed without incident. Exams requested:
- Request an immune deficiency assessment for the sister as part of the screening
